children.name: ValidateCommand
children.purpose: Check that the command is one of the allowed values and that required fields in message_data are present for that command.
children.behavior: If command is not in ['send', 'history', 'create_channel', 'join'], return error. For each command, verify required fields exist in message_data (e.g., channel_id for send/history/join, channel_name for create_channel, user_id for all).

semantic_inputs.name: message_data
semantic_inputs.description: Payload with fields like content, channel_id, user_id, channel_name

semantic_outputs.name: validation_result
semantic_outputs.description: Object with success flag and error message if invalid

postconditions: If invalid, parent returns error immediately
guarantees: Only valid commands and required fields proceed
children.stop_reason: Pure validation function with no further decomposition needed.

children.name: ExecuteSend
children.purpose: Handle the 'send' command: validate membership, store message, update user's last_seen, notify channel members.
children.behavior: Check if user is member of channel via channels data. If not, return error. If yes, generate message_id, store message in messages store, update user's last_seen in users store, and prepare notification data for other members.

semantic_outputs.name: send_result
semantic_outputs.description: Object with success flag, data (message_id), and message

children.stop_reason: Can be further decomposed into membership check, store, update, notify.

children.name: ExecuteHistory
children.purpose: Handle the 'history' command: retrieve last 100 messages from a channel sorted by timestamp descending.
children.behavior: Query messages store for all messages with given channel_id, sort by timestamp descending, limit to 100, return list.

semantic_outputs.name: history_result
semantic_outputs.description: Object with success flag, data (list of messages), and message

children.stop_reason: Pure query operation with no further decomposition needed.

children.name: ExecuteCreateChannel
children.purpose: Handle the 'create_channel' command: create new channel with unique name, set caller as creator and first member.
children.behavior: Check if channel name already exists in channels store. If yes, return error. If no, generate channel_id, create channel entry with members list containing user_id, store in channels.

semantic_outputs.name: create_channel_result
semantic_outputs.description: Object with success flag, data (channel_id), and message

children.stop_reason: Atomic operation with uniqueness check and creation.

children.name: ExecuteJoin
children.purpose: Handle the 'join' command: add user to channel's member list if not already a member.
children.behavior: Check if user_id is already in channel's members list. If yes, return error. If no, add user_id to members list and update channels store.

semantic_outputs.name: join_result
semantic_outputs.description: Object with success flag, data (updated member list), and message

children.stop_reason: Simple membership update with duplicate check.

decomposition_rationale: The parent ChatApp function is decomposed into one validation child that checks command and required fields, and four command-specific execution children (send, history, create_channel, join). Each child handles exactly one command, ensuring clear separation of concerns and minimal overlap. The parent orchestrates by first calling ValidateCommand, then based on the command, calling the appropriate execution child.
